title: Selaginella anceps
taxonomy: Selaginella anceps is classified in the division Pteridophyta, which is a group of vascular plants that produce spores instead of seeds. It belongs to the class Lycopodiopsida, a group of lycopods that are characterized by their simple, non-branching stems. Further, it falls under the order Selaginellales, which is a distinct order of lycopods. The family to which it belongs is Selaginellaceae, which is a family of lycopods that are primarily found in tropical regions. At the species level, Selaginella anceps is a distinct species within the genus Selaginella.
